top_signals.title: Houthis Declare Naval Blockade on Saudi Arabia as Red Sea Crisis Deepens
top_signals.summary: Yemen’s Iran-aligned Houthis declared an immediate naval blockade on Saudi Arabia on Monday, Reuters reported, bringing the U.S.-Iran war officially to the southern entrance of the Red Sea and threatening an export route used by Riyadh to bypass disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.  The group said the “maritime embargo” was retaliation for what it described as a Saudi siege of Yemen, Reuters reported. Saudi Arabia had not responded to the announcement as of the time of writing. Iran had previously instructed the Houthis to…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Latin America Outperforms as Emerging Market Currencies Gain
top_signals.summary: Emerging-market currencies gained to start the week, with Latin American names getting a boost from a drop in volatility which further boosts their carry appeal.

top_signals.title: Jim Cramer says it's time to look beyond tech as AI uncertainty rattles the market
top_signals.summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer says the AI trade has become too unpredictable, making this a better time to put new money into high-quality companies outside of technology.

top_signals.title: Testing times require time for testing − speech by Ruth Smith
Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
top_signals.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.
